understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to complete each level within a limited step budget by maneuvering the selectable piece(s) around the grid—likely to reach goal tiles (marked +) and/or to arrange objects so a goal condition is satisfied (e.g., a selectable on a goal).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given in pixel coordinates with a 3-pixels-per-cell grid, and MOVE actions attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell in the chosen direction. If an adjacent object in that direction was pushable (e.g., a block), the move could push it along provided space/interaction rules allowed. Walls (#) acted as blockers, but some boundaries could have non-uniform behavior, so a blocked hint did not always mean the move would fail. Multiple selectables could exist: using CLICK on the exact on-screen coordinate of another selectable switched control to that piece (changing player.id/position) without moving pieces through space, consuming steps like other actions.
